example.en: Today the weather is quite nice.
example.es: Hoy el clima está bastante agradable.

notes:
khá means quite / fairly / pretty — stronger than “a little” but not as strong as “very.” It shows a medium‑high level.

It usually goes before adjectives or verbs to describe degree.  

With adjectives, it describes quality or level. With verbs, it means doing something to a fairly good degree.  

Stronger than hơi (a bit), weaker than rất (very) — think of it as the comfortable middle.
exampleSentencesTranslated: This movie is quite good so I want to watch it again.
Today I feel quite tired after working all day.
She speaks English quite well and communicates confidently.
exampleSentences: Bộ phim này khá hay nên anh muốn xem lại lần nữa.
Hôm nay em cảm thấy khá mệt sau khi làm việc cả ngày.
Chị ấy nói tiếng Anh khá tốt và giao tiếp tự tin.
notes_md:
**khá** means *quite / fairly / pretty* — stronger than “a little” but not as strong as “very.” It shows a medium‑high level.

It usually goes **before adjectives or verbs** to describe degree.  

With adjectives, it describes quality or level. With verbs, it means doing something to a fairly good degree.  

Stronger than **hơi** (a bit), weaker than **rất** (very) — think of it as the comfortable middle.
